title: TA 104 - Story Through Word and Image
description: Universal and archetypal principles of story with an emphasis on using images and words for creating stories for theatre and film.

title: FILM 103 - Art of Film: Beginning to 1950s
description: Motion picture as popular art. Contributions of individual artists in historical contexts.

title: FILM 104 - Art of Film: 1950s to Present
description: Motion picture as popular art. Contributions of individual artists in their historical contexts.

title: FILM 210 - Screenwriting I
description: Writing short scripts and treatments for indie experimental, documentary, and narrative films using 3-Act structure and story-craft. Developed scripts and treatments are offered to production courses.

title: FILM 215 - Filmmaking I
description: Introduction to fundamentals of filmmaking.

title: FILM 308 - Topics in Media Art History
description:
Topics in media art history from beginning to the present.
Cross-listed with: ART 308

title: FILM 375 - Filmmaking II
description: Intermediate course expanding on fundamentals of lighting, cinematography, digital editing and audio field production. Create short fiction and nonfiction films.

title: FILM 385 - Filmmaking III
description: Advanced film production. Students produce short films that include sound mixing, color correction, and graphics.

title: FILM 475 - Filmmaking IV
description: Capstone course. Students pitch, develop, shoot, and complete a short film. Basic distribution materials developed.

title: FILM 476 - Filmmaking V
description: Continued capstone work on student film developed in Film IV. Students pitch, develop, shoot, and complete a short film. Basic distribution materials developed.

title: FILM 494W - Professional Practices in Film
description: Development and refinement of professional practices and materials for careers in film, digital media and related fields.

title: ART 250 - Darkroom Photography
description: Fundamentals of fine art analog black-and-white photography. Camera operations, film development, and silver gelatin printing. Discuss work of historical and contemporary fine art photographers.

title: ART 251 - Photography I
description: Fundamentals of digital camera functions and introduction to digital imaging software. Exploration of creative image control and visual communication through lens-based image making.

title: FILM 260 - Film Festival
description: Pre-screenings and behind-the-scenes activities for the world’s oldest student-run film festival that will deepen sociopolitical understanding and provide insights to contemporary short film processes, aesthetics, and constructs.

title: FILM 350 - Screenwriting II
description: Advanced writing for short scripts and treatments for indie experimental, documentary, and narrative films using 3-Act structure and story-craft. Developed scripts and treatments are offered to production courses.

title: FILM 362 - Documentary Production
description: Fundamentals of ethical, non-fiction video production.

title: FILM 378 - Film/Digital Production Workshop
description: Special topics in film and/or digital production. Structure and curriculum varies.

title: FILM 425 - Film Directing and Production Processes
description: Students examine professional directing practices for the moving image, including production processes every director must master.

title: FILM 455 - Grant Writing
description: Fundamental practices of proposal development and grant writing; applicable to all professions. Hands-on activities as grantee and grantor. Emphasis on post-graduation grant writing. Includes working with a fiscal agent.

title: FILM 465 - Film Seminar
description: Seminar on film-related topics.

title: FILM 478 - Advanced Film/Digital Production Workshop
description: Special advanced topics in film and/or digital production. Structure and curriculum varies.

title: FILM 480 - Special Topics in Film
description: Variable topics. Check with department for upcoming topics.
